steps.title: Test the first pair
steps.content:
Substitute pair I, $(1,1)$, into $5x-3y<4$.
$$5(1)-3(1)=2$$
Since $2<4$, pair I satisfies the inequality.

steps.title: Test the second pair
steps.content:
For pair II, $(2,5)$, evaluate the left side.
$$5(2)-3(5)=-5$$
Since $-5<4$, pair II satisfies the inequality.

steps.title: Test the third pair
steps.content:
For pair III, $(3,2)$, evaluate the left side.
$$5(3)-3(2)=9$$
Since $9<4$ is false, pair III does not satisfy the inequality.

steps.title: Select the statement
steps.content: Only pairs I and II satisfy the inequality, and pair III does not. The answer is C.
